compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Provenance an auditor can't see isn't useful. The gate-code version+SHA stamp
(stories 1-2) must be visible where attestations are inspected (epic
jira-reb-596).

## What

- Surface `manifest_rebar_version()` output in the `show` attestation rendering
  (attestation data rides in the ticket state dict; render it in the show path in
  `src/rebar/_reads.py` / `src/rebar/__init__.py` alongside the certified/verdict
  block, HMAC still stripped).
- Add the stamp to `verify_signature`'s return payload AND its typed contract:
  the `VerifySignatureResultOut` model in `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` and the JSON
  schema `src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json` (new **nullable
  string** field, e.g. `rebar_version`).
- Pre-stamp manifests -> field is `None` / omitted; render nothing (no crash).

## Scope

Read/display surface + typed-contract extension only. Consumes story 1's parser.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar show <id>` displays the gate-code version+SHA for a stamped
      attestation.
- [ ] `verify_signature` returns the stamp; `VerifySignatureResultOut` and
      `verify_signature_result.schema.json` are extended with the nullable field.
- [ ] A pre-stamp attestation shows/returns no stamp (field `None`) without error.
- [ ] Tests cover `show` AND `verify_signature` for stamped and pre-stamp
      attestations, including the schema/model validation of the new field.

file_impact.reason: render stamp in show attestation block

compiled_state.title: Surface rebar-version stamp in show + verify_signature
